short: A 16-bit brain floating point format widely used in modern model training.
definition: BF16 is a 16-bit floating point format, short for brain floating point, that keeps the same exponent range as 32-bit float while using fewer bits for precision. The wide range avoids the overflow problems of older 16-bit formats, making training more stable. It is common in modern large-model training because it halves memory and bandwidth with little accuracy loss.
